name: HMS ML 140 (ML 140)
events: 12 Sep 1944 Around 0930 B/12, HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) arrived at Heysham. She was escorted in by HMML 145 (T/Lt. G.S. Sykes, RNVR) which had joined around 0810 B/12.
She departed later the same day to participate in exercises off Morecambe Bay. HMML 145 again being in company. (1)

18 Sep 1944 HMS Uproar (Lt. J.N. Devlin, DSC, RN) proceeded to Douglas, Isle of Man upon completion of the exercises off Morecambe Bay. She arrived at Douglas around 0940 B/18 being escorted to there by HMML 145 (T/Lt. G.S. Sykes, RNVR) which had joined around 0700 A/18.
Around 1230 A/18, she departed Douglas to return to the exercise area. HMML 145 and HMML 152 (T/S.Lt. T.O. Roberts, RNVR) being in company as escorts.
At 1925 A/18, she entered Douglas harbour once more escorted by HMML 152. (1)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
